transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Hello and welcome to On the Record. Those of you who joined us last week were probably

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: amazed, as I was, by some of the latest research in the field of UFOs. There is so much to talk

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: about, however, we were unable to fit it all into one program, so my guest again this week

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: is UFO researcher, former state senate candidate, and award-winning pilot John Lear. Mr. Lear,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: nice to have you here again. Always a pleasure to be here, George. To begin with, we have

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: this concept of the people from other planets as little green men. Are they green, from what

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: you know? Are they all the same, or we got different kinds of them? George, there's at least 70

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: different species, and probably more. But yes, as odd as it sounds, there are little green

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: men. That's not the ones we usually see, though. We see the little gray ones. They're about

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: four and a half feet tall. We see the ones that are called the Nordics. They're about

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: seven feet tall, look like us, but they're invariably blonde hair and blue-eyed. They're

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: one of the species that we have on ice. One of their crashes we recovered. Obviously, with

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: different species, they have different motives. I mean, are there good aliens and bad aliens,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: from what you know? Good aliens and bad aliens, and all kinds

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: of ways and reasons for interacting with us. What are some of the reasons? Why would they

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: be here? Genetic experiments, trying to, you know, there's a misconception here. People think that

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: our space brothers would bring us, you know, good things and teach us how to get rid of all our

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: diseases. And, you know, if you get to, if you stop and think about it, that may not be the best

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: thing. Because if you had a society without any disease and everybody starts living 150 years,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: that creates a lot of problems. So they would not meddle in that way. You believe, in part, that the

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: movies, E.T., Close Encounters of the Third Kind, were not entirely just the creative products of some

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Hollywood director, that maybe there's more to that. Why don't you explain? They were carefully guided by

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: representatives from MJ-12, who is this organization that is in charge of the cover-up, in order to get

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: us ready for the release of the information that there are indeed aliens. And in both those movies,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: they used small little creatures in order to get us ready for this release of information.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: To prepare us that the idea of benevolent aliens from outer space are going to help us out.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Unfortunately, they're not so benevolent. So they made probably one of the most

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: disastrous mistakes when E.T. was put out. At that time was before we discovered what I call the

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Double Cross, or the Grand Deception. And they were trying to get us used to the benevolent little

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: creatures. But that isn't the case. Why don't you take a couple of minutes? Now, you've done as

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: much research on UFOs as just about anyone, I'd imagine. And if you haven't done it, you know people

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: who have. Where did it all start? And how have we put this picture together of where they came from and why they're here?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Well, the whole thing started in July 2, 1947. That was the first crash.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: First crash of an alien spaceship. Of an alien spaceship. July 6 is when they recovered it and sent it to Wright-Patterson Air Force base.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: July 7 is when they held the press conference and told the people it was a weather balloon, which the press bought hook, line, and sinker.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: September of that year, President Truman established MJ-12. And the purpose of that was to study the saucers and cover up the existence of UFOs.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: And you say we recovered alien bodies from that incident.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Four from that particular crash, yes. And what did they do with them?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: They autopsied them. Dr. Detlef Bronck was the chief surgeon, so to speak. He was the one that named them EBEs.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: It stands for Extraterrestrial Biological Entities. And we captured three live EBEs. They were numbered in their order of capture.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: EBE-1, EBE-2, and EBE-3. Where did this happen?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: In different crashes. EBE-1 lived with an Air Force colonel between 1949 and 1952.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: The EBEs are kept in an electromagnetic facility called a YY-2.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: It was designed by a Dr. Eric Wang, who worked directly for Dr. Kissinger, who has been in this cover-up since the very beginning.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: The reason they had to create an electromagnetic facility is the fact that the EBEs are so advanced.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: They're at least a half a billion years advanced from us, and probably more. They can move by thought.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Move by thought.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Thought. And they can also disappear. But they can't move by thought through electromagnetic facilities.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: So they built this facility. There was two, one in Los Alamos, and one either at Edwards or up at the test site.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: At Groom Lake, Nevada.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: At Groom Lake. Now, there's still one EBE still living.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: EBE-3, and there is a videotape that eventually is going to be released to the public, and it's an interview with him.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: As hard as that may sound, as hard as that may be to believe, there is a videotape of an interview with EBE-3.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: And what is this language that the interviewers are hearing?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: I haven't seen the tape, so I don't know.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: But on this trip, I told you I took, gathering all this information, a guy in Albuquerque handed me 25 secret CIA memos.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: And if anything can be humorous in all of this, one of these memos, in the body of the text, it said,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: By the way, EBE-3 wants to see the ocean. We better make the arrangements or he'll disappear again.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: So while he couldn't get out of this facility, he could make himself disappear and cause him a lot of trouble.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Now, can they exist among us? Is that what you're saying? Or do they have to have this special environment?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: They have a special environment, special air. It's my understanding they can only last about 20 minutes in this air.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: You were saying also these gray ones are the ones that we see most of the time, and you said that there are a lot of different motives for them being here.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: The gray ones, what kind of a deal we got going with them? Is that the bunch that you suggest we've made a deal with for military technology?

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Well, I think we made a deal with them, and their ultimate, what they want to do is regenerate their own race.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Now, apparently, they've either had some kind of nuclear accident, or they're on the backside of an evolutionary genetic curve,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: and they're going downhill instead of uphill. In other words, in the autopsies, of which I have two, we found out that their digestive system is atrophied.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: They can't eat through their mouth anymore. You know how they eat? Well, these cattle mutilations, they take the hormones and the enzymes from those cattle,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: and they spread it on their skin, and their skin absorbs the nutrients and then excretes the waste back through their skin.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: And if you read Whitley Streber's book, or listen to him talk, he talks about when he's seen them doing this to their skin, and they're scraping the waste off.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Now, these cattle mutilations, there's been a film made about that, and perhaps in a couple of minutes, we can take a look at some excerpts of that.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: Yeah, the Strange Harvest was a good one, but another thing that happened here on March 4th, there was a documentary released on the best UFO photos that have been taken so far,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: including a minute and 38 seconds of videotape. Now, what happened is in November, this guy took these photos, took it into the TV station,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: they thought it was great, but nobody would believe it, so they spent three months checking the validity of the,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: checking the, authenticating the videotape, and checking out the credibility of the witness, and when they had it all down,

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: they put out this documentary, 30 minutes, I mean, it's ironclad, they're here, but do you think it got on the network? No, it was just showed locally in Pensacola.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: We have some of that, and perhaps when we come back, we can take a look at it, so stay with us for more of On The Record.

transcript_title: Ebe 3 John Lear Ebe 3 At 4 Min
text: On The Record.
